LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LITTLE CAESAR ENTERPRISES, INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE, IF PLAINTIFF CAN IN GOOD FAITH, THAT THE MANUFACTURER OF THE DOUGH SHEETER "DESIGNED, INSTALLED, OR OTHERWISE REQUIRED BY SPECIFICATION FOR THE ATTACHMENT OF GUARDS AND CONVEYED KNOWLEDGE OF THE SAME TO THE EMPLOYER," AS REQUIRED BY LABOR CODE 4558(C). (SEE ALSO AWARD METALS INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1991) 228 CAL.APP.3D 1128, 1134.) THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT ("TAC") ADEQUATELY PLEADS FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT A REASONABLE PERSON COULD FIND THAT THE DOUGH SHEETER FALLS UNDER THE DEFINITION OF "POWER PRESS" IN LAB. CODE SEC. 4558. (TAC, 6A; SEE ISLAS V. D&G MANUFACTURING COMPANY, INC. (2004) 120 CAL.APP.4TH 571, HOLDING WHERE THERE ARE TWO REASONABLE INTERPRETATIONS OF WHETHER A MACHINE IS A "POWER PRESS" UNDER LABOR CODE SEC. 4558, IT IS A QUESTION OF FACT FOR THE JURY TO DECIDE; SEE ALSO ROSALES V. DEPUTY ACE MEDICAL COMPANY (2000) 22 CAL.4TH 279, 286, HOLDING THAT A POWER PRESS IS A MACHINE THAT UTILIZES "THE USE OF A TOOL THAT IMPARTS SHAPE TO MATERIAL BY PRESSING OR IMPACTING AGAINST OR THROUGH THE MATERIAL, THAT IS, BY PUNCHING, STAMPING, OR EXTRUDING.") PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT LITTLE CASEAR ENTERPRISES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. IN RULING ON A DEMURRER, THE COMPLAINT MUST BE "LIBERALLY CONSTRUED, WITH A VIEW TO SUBSTANTIAL JUSTICE BETWEEN THE PARTIES." (CCP SEC. 452.) A GENERAL DEMURRER SHOULD BE SUSTAINED "ONLY IF THE COMPLAINT FAILS TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION UNDER ANY POSSIBLE LEGAL THEORY." (SHEENHAN V. SAN FRANCISCO 49ERS, LTD. (2009) 45 CAL.4TH 992, 998.) LABOR CODE SEC. 4558(C) REQUIRES THE PLAINTIFF TO PROVE THAT THE MANUFACTURER OF THE POWER PRESS CONVEYED THE NECESSITY OF THE GUARDS TO THE EMPLOYER. THE FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT ("FAC") PLEADS THAT THE MANUFACTURER "DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY" NOTIFIED DEFENDANT ABOUT THE NEED FOR THE GUARD. (FAC, PAR. 22.) THE FAC ALSO PLEADS THAT THE USE AND OPERATIONS MANUAL CONTAINED INFORMATION ABOUT THE NECESSITY OF THE GUARD. (FAC, PAR. 22.) LIBERALLY CONSTRUING THE ALLEGATIONS, THE FAC STATES A CAUSE OF ACTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED. (302)

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS; PLAINTIFF NEGINA MOHAMMAD's Motion For Order Compelling Reponses To Request For Production Of Documents is Granted, in part, and denied, in part. Motion to compel further response to plaintiff's document request granted to the extent defendant shall provide a verified response addressing the existence/non-existence of electronic or digital documents and produce same to the extent they exist. Defendant shall produce a witness to testify concerning the record retention policy, the location of the premises where the documents were stored, the facts concerning the water damage to the records, what happened to the damaged records and where the damaged records are currently stored. The motion in all other respects is denied. If further discovery reveals the existence of responsive documents, a more specifically framed document request may be served. Sanction was denied by the court. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CRC 3.1312. Judge: John Stewart; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ashley Soevyn, CSR #12019

DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-15-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT LITTLE CASEAR ENTERPRISES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. In ruling on a demurrer, the complaint must be "liberally construed, with a view to substantial justice between the parties." (CCP sec. 452.) A general demurrer should be sustained "only if the complaint fails to state a cause of action under any possible legal theory." (Sheenhan v. San Francisco 49ers, Ltd. (2009) 45 Cal.4th 992, 998.) Labor Code sec. 4558(c) requires the plaintiff to prove that the manufacturer of the power press conveyed the necessity of the guards to the employer. The Fourth Amended Complaint ("FAC") pleads that the manufacturer "directly or indirectly" notified Defendant about the need for the guard. (FAC, par. 22.) The FAC also pleads that the Use and Operations Manual contained information about the necessity of the guard. (FAC, par. 22.) Liberally construing the allegations, the FAC states a cause of action. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

The Third Amended Complaint ("TAC") adequately pleads facts establishing that a reasonable person could find that the dough sheeter falls under the definition of "Power Press" in Lab. Code sec. 4558. (TAC, 6a; See Islas v. D&G Manufacturing Company, Inc. (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 571, holding where there are two reasonable interpretations of whether a machine is a "power press" under Labor Code sec. 4558, it is a question of fact for the jury to decide; see also Rosales v. Deputy Ace Medical Company (2000) 22 Cal.4th 279, 286, holding that a power press is a machine that utilizes "the use of a tool that imparts shape to material by pressing or impacting against or through the material, that is, by punching, stamping, or extruding.")
